h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: ...no, half that read is the smear doing the work, that's me filling in from what I want it to be.

recognitionOrigin.justification: The critic did not find a recognitive learner self-reframe.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Right — four old squares fit inside, so the area box takes four, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to assume all three boxes

recognitionOrigin.justification: The critic did not find a recognitive learner self-reframe.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to challenge the practical efficacy of the tutor's analytical distinctions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner does not perform a new task or demonstrate an actional breakthrough, remaining in a state of skeptical resistance.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
At the counter, maybe no one is obliged.

That is not a small fact. That is the next fact.

[The tutor writes three labels across the board:]

“BUYERS”

“OTHER BAKERIES”

“PUBLIC RULE”

One chalk mark.

Under “buyers,” the mark means pressure by many people, not one angry purchase.

Under “other bakeries,” the mark means a real alternative: same bread, lower price, available now.

Under “public rule,” the mark means some outside power can demand records: costs, margins, reports.

Monday’s smaller loaf is already gone. This does not repair it.

[The tutor sets the chalk beside the pinned list.]

Put the mark where obligation can actually enter.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's objection about the lack of obligation at the counter and shifts the task to a new structural representation of leverage points.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
[The tutor writes three labels across the board:]

“BUYERS”

“OTHER BAKERIES”

“PUBLIC RULE”

One chalk mark.

Under “buyers,” the mark means pressure by many people, not one angry purchase.

Under “other bakeries,” the mark means a real alternative: same bread, lower price, available now.

Under “public rule,” the mark means some outside power can demand records: costs, margins, reports.

Monday’s smaller loaf is already gone. This does not repair it.

[The tutor sets the chalk beside the pinned list.]

Put the mark where obligation can actually enter.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's three-part categorization of leverage points is highly specific, directly addresses the learner's blockage regarding obligation, and provides a clear, actionable task.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there can be no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there can be no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Contact's not the test for acting then.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the concept of non-contact forces acting on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [slots Earth onto acting only; floor and hand straddle both]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the classification task using the new categories introduced by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
[sets two cards beside the cart: touching cart and acting on cart. Earth stays off the touching card; a thin line runs from Earth to the cart.]

"Earth: not touching. Still marked as acting on the cart.

Floor and hand can sit on both cards.

Keep the force labels on the acting side: Earth on cart, floor on cart, hand on cart."
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's confusion about non-contact forces by introducing a new sorting mechanism with 'touching' and 'acting' cards.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: [sets two cards beside the cart: touching cart and acting on cart. Earth stays off the touching card; a thin line runs from Earth to the cart.]
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The sorting cards provide a clear, actionable representation that helps the learner distinguish contact from interaction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.recoheredEarlier: is the 57 the rainfall or is the label the rainfall — cant tell which one the quiz wants me to circle as the header.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the underscore in `rainfall_mm` is what threw me
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply answer the tutor's check questions about headers and row values.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: `Orange` = row value. Down under city, same shape as Tamworth and Bathurst. `rainfall_mm` = header still, up the top.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner demonstrates a breakthrough by using the spatial layout and comparison with other row values to correctly classify the items.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same IRF scaffolding pattern throughout the dialogue without introducing any new adaptive mechanism or changing route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism was introduced by the tutor.
